subject: Chemistry
title: Chinese Alchemy and Elixirs of Life
description: A quick, practical guide to Chinese Alchemy and Elixirs of Life.

hook.text: Chinese alchemists weren't chasing gold. They wanted immortality—and they accidentally invented gunpowder.

buildup.text: Daoist alchemists sought the 'elixir of life' by mixing mercury, sulfur, and other minerals.

discovery.text: Emperor Qin Shi Huang drank mercury-based elixirs believing they'd make him immortal. They killed him instead.

twist.text: Around 850 AD, alchemists mixed saltpeter, sulfur, and charcoal. Instead of immortality, they got an explosion.

climax.text: Gunpowder changed warfare forever. The alchemists' greatest failure became one of history's most important inventions.

punchline.text: Seeking immortality, they found gunpowder. Alchemy's best accidents.

quiz.question: What did Chinese alchemists accidentally invent while searching for the elixir of life?
